architecture_summary: A microservices e-commerce application with an API Gateway fronting multiple services (Auth, Orders, Payments, Notifications). PostgreSQL and Redis are used for persistence. An admin dashboard communicates directly with internal services. External payment processor integration visible.

threats.title: JWT token forgery at API Gateway
threats.description: If the API Gateway uses a weak or symmetric JWT signing key, an attacker could forge valid tokens and impersonate any user, including administrators.
mitigations: Use RS256 (asymmetric) signing algorithm for JWT tokens
Implement short token expiration (15 minutes) with refresh token rotation
Add token revocation list using Redis-based blacklist
Validate audience (aud) and issuer (iss) claims on every request

threats.title: Order data manipulation via unprotected internal API
threats.description: The Orders Service API appears to have no authentication for internal calls. A compromised container could tamper with order records by making direct API calls.
mitigations: Implement mTLS between all internal microservices
Add service mesh with per-service identity (e.g., Istio/Linkerd)
Sign critical order events with HMAC before persisting

threats.title: No audit trail for payment transactions
threats.description: Payment processing flows are not logged with immutable audit records, allowing disputes where neither party can prove what occurred.
mitigations: Implement append-only audit log for all payment events
Use digital signatures on payment confirmation messages
Store logs in tamper-evident storage (e.g., Azure Immutable Blob Storage)

threats.title: Unencrypted inter-service communication exposes PII
threats.description: HTTP (not HTTPS) connections between services could expose customer PII (names, addresses, payment card data) in plaintext on the internal network.
mitigations: Enforce TLS 1.2+ for all inter-service communication
Encrypt sensitive fields at the application level (AES-256)
Use VNet service endpoints to restrict database access
Enable PostgreSQL SSL mode = require

threats.title: Redis cache stores sensitive session data without encryption
threats.description: Session tokens and user profile data cached in Redis are stored in plaintext. A compromised Redis instance exposes all active sessions.
mitigations: Enable Redis AUTH and TLS
Encrypt sensitive values before caching (application-level encryption)
Set appropriate TTL on all session keys
Use Redis ACL to restrict command access

threats.title: No rate limiting on public API Gateway
threats.description: The API Gateway has no visible rate limiting configuration. An attacker can flood endpoints with requests, exhausting compute resources and making the platform unavailable for legitimate customers.
mitigations: Implement rate limiting (e.g., 100 req/min per IP, 1000/min per authenticated user)
Add Azure DDoS Protection Standard
Configure WAF rules for common attack patterns
Implement circuit breakers between services

threats.title: Database connection pool exhaustion
threats.description: Without connection pool limits, a surge of requests or a slow query attack can exhaust PostgreSQL connections, causing cascading failures across all services.
mitigations: Configure connection pool limits per service (e.g., PgBouncer)
Set query timeouts at application and database level
Implement async processing for non-critical database operations

threats.title: Admin dashboard has no network isolation
threats.description: The admin dashboard is accessible from the same network as customer-facing services with no separate trust boundary. A compromised customer account or SSRF vulnerability could be used to access admin functionality.
mitigations: Place admin dashboard behind VPN or private endpoint only
Implement separate authentication flow for admin users (MFA required)
Add IP allowlisting for admin routes at the Gateway level
Use separate deployment with dedicated service principal

recommendations: CRITICAL: Enforce TLS for all inter-service communication immediately — customer PII is at risk
CRITICAL: Isolate the admin dashboard behind VPN with mandatory MFA
HIGH: Implement mTLS and service mesh for internal service authentication
HIGH: Add rate limiting and DDoS protection to the public API Gateway
HIGH: Enable Redis TLS and AUTH; encrypt sensitive session data before caching
